channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Uncertainty Remains Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iranian militaries have offered conflicting accounts over whether the crucial waterway is open.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz continues to be a point of negotiation between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: As Vance Offers Iran ‘Outstretched Hand’ in Talks, Trump Threatens Bombing
item.description: The administration’s split message came as the United States and Iran began a new round of nuclear negotiations in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Steve Witkoff, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance and the Pakistani Army official Asim Munir at Lake Lucerne in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Have Plenty to Negotiate Beyond Nuclear Issues
item.description: Both sides have committed to discussing sanctions relief and the shape of a reconstruction fund, as well as Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking at the Bürgenstock resort, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Netanyahu faces one of the biggest challenges of his career.
item.description: In a ‘plot twist’, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appears to be heading into an election this fall without his main prop: President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel during a news conference in Jerusalem this week.

item.title: Three Men Are Key to Iran’s Approach to U.S. Talks
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader, foreign minister and parliament speaker all play central roles in hammering out an agreement with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, center, Iran’s foreign minister, arriving near Lucerne, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Israel Directs Its Military to Limit Its Actions in Lebanon, but Tensions Persist
item.description: It was not clear whether the new directive would resolve the friction that led to deadly clashes on Friday and Saturday and threatened to derail a preliminary U.S.-Iran peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Christina Goldbaum
item.media:description: Damage from Israeli strike in Nabatieh, Lebanon, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Nuclear Talks Expected to Hinge on These 4 Questions
item.description: Tehran insists that its nuclear program is for peaceful purposes. The Trump administration is demanding assurances that Iran cannot secretly develop a weapon.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, on Sunday at the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland.

item.title: A Glamorous Swiss Resort Hosts Officials for U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: The Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne has hosted some of the world’s wealthiest and most powerful people for more than 100 years.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Bürgenstock resort above Lake Lucerne in Switzerland has hosted some of the world’s wealthiest and most powerful people over nearly 150 years.

item.title: Neither the War Nor Trump’s Deal Terminated the Main Threats in Iran, Analysts Say
item.description: Neither the war nor the agreement terminated the main threats emanating from Iran, many analysts said.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A painting of Iran’s first two supreme leaders in Tehran this month. The second, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed at the start of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran this year.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Ephrat Livni, Aurelien Breeden, Isabel Kershner and Yan Zhuang

item.title: U.S. and Iranian Officials to Meet for Peace Talks in Switzerland
item.description: Amid a volley of threats, the brief talks largely focused on the war in Lebanon, according to Iranian state media.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A plane carrying an Iranian negotiating team landed at Zurich International Airport in Switzerland on Saturday.

item.title: Mona Khalil, Defender of Sea Turtles, Killed in an Israeli Strike in Lebanon
item.description: For a quarter century, Ms. Khalil ran a guesthouse and worked to protect endangered sea turtles who every summer lay their eggs on a stretch of beach near Tyre, Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai
item.category: Society for the Protection of Nature in Lebanon
item.media:description: Mona Khalil in Mansuri, Lebanon, in 2004.

item.title: Canada’s postcard moment at the World Cup is more than the team’s victory.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Nathan Saliba, a player with Canada’s team who replaced the midfielder Ismaël Koné after his injury, held up a team jersey with his teammate’s surname and number, after scoring a goal.

item.title: New Strait of Hormuz Closure Announcement Threatens the Slow Uptick in Traffic
item.description: Fifty-five ships had passed through the strait on Saturday, the U.S. military said. But then Iran’s military said it was closing the waterway once again.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Are Still Fighting, Several Cease-Fires Later
item.description: For the second time in two days, Lebanon was again at the center of whether the fragile U.S.-Iran deal would hold.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Qannarit village, southern Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Shipping remains far below prewar levels in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description: A preliminary U.S.-Iran deal took steps to reopen the vital waterway, but traffic remains erratic.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Alan Yuhas

item.title: Mines, Logistics and Deep Uncertainty Threaten a Middle East Oil Rebound
item.description: More oil is getting out of the Persian Gulf, but the region’s producers are looking for signs that it is safe as they ramp up plans for alternative routes.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels, including oil tankers, anchored this week in the waters off Muscat, Oman, near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Lebanon Emerges as Weak Link in U.S.-Iran Deal to End War
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, once seen as a secondary front to the American-Israeli war on Iran, has become one of the main obstacles to ending it.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Outside the grave of the former Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah, who was killed in an Israeli strike, in Beirut on Tuesday.

item.title: Lebanon Cease-Fire Bolsters U.S.-Iran Truce, After a Shaky Start
item.description: Talks on a long-term peace deal were set to begin on Friday, but Iran pulled out, citing Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Hours later, diplomats say, Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt fighting there.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Euan Ward, Adam Rasgon, Johnatan Reiss and Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cleaning up after Israeli strikes in Touline, southern Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Deal Is Off to a Chaotic Start
item.description: The preliminary agreement, not yet a week old, has sown confusion: nixed Swiss signing ceremonies, rifts with Israel and Congress, and uncertainty over new talks.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland, where Swiss officials had hoped to host new peace negotiations between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Says He Dislikes Deal With U.S., but Allowed It
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei distanced himself from the agreement, putting responsibility for it on Iran’s president, and said it does not mean acceding to U.S. demands.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Photos of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, are held up during a memorial for victims of the war, in Tehran in May.

item.title: Our Allies Are Wondering Whether Supporting the American War Machine Is Worth It
item.description: Is the Iran war an early sign of “superpower suicide"?
item.dc:creator: Linda Kinstler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Deadly Violence Rocks Lebanon in Threat to U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: Israel launched strikes across the country after Iran-backed Hezbollah said it had ambushed Israeli troops, leaving diplomats scrambling to prevent the deal from collapsing.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke following Israeli bombardment in southern Lebanon on Friday.

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as U.S.-Iran Deal Faces Tests
item.description: Oil remains on track for a second straight weekly decline, but signs of cracks in the agreement to end the war have given traders pause.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Stranded Ships Hoping for Escape Find a Turbulent Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Shipping companies had hoped to get their vessels out after this week’s U.S.-Iran deal but faced uncertainty on Friday as violence flared again in the region.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Thursday.

item.title: How Deal With U.S. Could Reconnect Iran to the Global Economy
item.description: The country has long faced some of the world’s heaviest sanctions. The lifting of restrictions could open new vistas on trade and financial access.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Street vendors in Tehran this week. Years of sanctions and economic mismanagement in Iran have driven inflation and unemployment higher.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Adam Rasgon, Johnatan Reiss and Euan Ward

item.title: Vance’s Defense of Iran Deal Rests on Vague and Misleading Claims
item.description: The vice president said the United States had leverage to dictate the outcome of the next round of negotiations. But he claimed incorrectly that Iran got no new benefit from the lifting of oil sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Rebecca F. Elliott and Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during a briefing at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Vance Lashes Out at Israeli Critics of U.S.-Iran Agreement to End the War
item.description: The vice president said that the United States was the only powerful ally Israel had left and noted that two-thirds of the weapons that protected Israel were paid for by U.S. tax dollars.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan, Erica L. Green, Max Bearak and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance during a news briefing at the White House on Thursday. He lashed out at critics of President Trump’s deal with Iran.

item.title: Iran and the U.S. Have an Understanding. Will It Lead to a Deal?
item.description: Europe and the larger world will be watching carefully to see if talks produce a lasting agreement on Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: President Trump in Évian-les-Bains, France, for the Group of 7 meeting on Wednesday.

item.title: Gulf States Are Frustrated by Failure to Tackle Iran’s Missiles, Analysts Say
item.description: The preliminary U.S.-Iranian peace deal does not address Iranian rockets or drones, raising questions in the region about relying on Washington as a security guarantor.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard with a photo of a missile and a message saying “Every missile has one message” in Tehran earlier this year.

item.title: Hegseth Berates NATO Allies for ‘Shameful’ Response to U.S. War in Iran
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a meeting of NATO defense ministers to continue the Trump administration’s sharp criticism of European military policies.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during a NATO meeting on Thursday.

item.title: Israel Stunned by Trump’s Iran Deal
item.description: The agreement accomplishes none of Israel’s stated war aims and arguably leaves the country in worse shape on each of them.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Max Bearak, Jonathan Swan and Rebecca F. Elliott

item.title: Iran Gets Major Economic Lifeline for Minimal Concessions in Initial Deal
item.description: The agreement delays the most difficult steps for Iran for later talks, while granting it crucial benefits.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A satellite image of Kharg Island, a crucial Iranian oil hub. The deal grants Iran waivers to begin exporting its oil even before the negotiation of a final agreement on its nuclear program.

item.title: Why Flight Prices Might Not Fall After the U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: Jet fuel may stay expensive for months, and airlines know travelers are willing to pay more for tickets.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Jet fuel prices nearly doubled after the war with Iran started, and they are unlikely to fall substantially for months, analysts said.

item.title: Average U.S. Gas Price Falls Below $4 After U.S.-Iran Agreement
item.description: Drivers are enjoying some relief at the pump after the United States and Iran signed an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Kailyn Rhone
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gasoline prices rose sharply after the start of U.S.-led war with Iran.

item.title: Team Medic Recounts Iran’s ‘Stressful’ World Cup Debut in L.A.
item.description: A member of Iran’s medical team says the team endured delays, multiple security searches and a rushed exit, frustrating players and officials.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s Saeid Ezatolahi receiving medical attention during the match against New Zealand at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on Monday.

item.title: Before Making a Deal, Trump Demanded Iran’s Surrender. He Got a Surprise.
item.description: While the Iranians suffered substantial losses in the war, they emerged from a confrontation with the world’s most powerful military having proved they can use economic chaos as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at a Group of 7 summit in France on Wednesday. The president has argued he has the upper hand with an agreement that leaves negotiations over Iran’s nuclear program for a later date.

item.title: How Does Trump’s Deal With Iran Compare to Obama’s?
item.description: The two agreements are difficult to compare, because the current memorandum of understanding is an interim arrangement meant to outline a negotiating path to a fuller deal.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Paris on Wednesday and headed a dinner with the French president, Emmanuel Macron, at the Palace of Versailles.

item.title: Trump Defends Deal to End the War With Iran as Details Emerge
item.description: President Trump lashed out at critics who say the agreement achieves less than the one President Barack Obama signed in 2015, and he threatened to bomb Iran again if it violated the deal.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Farnaz Fassihi and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and other U.S. officials at a news conference amid the Group of 7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Administration Shares Terms of Iran Deal Including Oil Export Waivers
item.description: The document leaves complicated issues like Iran’s nuclear program still to be decided and a 60-day window to determine them.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and other leaders of the Group of 7 countries in France on Wednesday. A senior official read the U.S. agreement with Iran to reporters as Mr. Trump held a news conference.

item.title: Stars of Israel’s TV Channel for Bibi Fans Turn on Trump
item.description: Leading figures from Channel 14, a right-wing broadcaster that supports Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, are openly attacking the U.S. president over his Iran deal.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Yinon Magal, the host of “The Patriots,” Channel 14’s combative flagship talk show, at the studio in 2023.

item.title: Allowing Iran to Charge Fees in Strait of Hormuz Would Set ‘Dangerous Precedent,’ Maersk CEO Says
item.description: With the United States and Iran nearing a deal to reopen the Persian Gulf, the shipping giant is treading cautiously.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Charlotte de la Fuente for The New York Times
item.media:description: “There’s been a couple of times where it was declared open and was not really open,” said Vincent Clerc, the chief executive of Maersk.

item.title: Stocks Drop on Fed Decision, and Oil Is Muted Amid Peace Deal Talks
item.description: Traders are waiting for U.S. and Iranian officials to meet in Switzerland on Friday, when they are expected to sign an initial agreement and begin a 60-day cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
